Effective_Date:01-Apr-2024: IS Audit FY 2023-24 - Database Security Review
RBI Master Direction on IS Audit compliance framework. Industry standards: ISO 27001:2022 A.8.24

Entity:Bharat Mercantile Bank Limited: Likelihood: 4.5/5 (Very Likely - default accounts are easily discoverable), Impact: 5.0/5 (Catastrophic - full DB access compromise)

Effective_Date:01-Apr-2024: P default administrative accounts enabled on production SQL servers with weak password policy

During the IS audit of database security controls for FY 2023-24, the audit team identified that the default "sa" (system administrator) account was enabled on 14 out of 22 production SQL Server instances supporting the bank's core banking system, internet banking platform, and enterprise data warehouse. The password for these accounts had not been rotated in over 18 months. Additionally, Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) was not configured for any database administrator (DBA) login. This configuration exposes the bank to unauthorized database access, privilege escalation, and potential data exfiltration from the bank's critical systems containing customer financial data, transaction records, and sensitive personal information.

Entity:Bharat Mercantile Bank Limited: No mandatory password rotation policy exists for database service accounts and privileged accounts at Bharat Mercantile Bank Limited. The existing password policy (POL-ISMS-040) covers user accounts only.

MFA enablement for DBA access was not included in the database hardening standard (STD-DB-012). The standard was last updated in 2021 and does not reference RBI guidelines on privileged access management.
Database administrators were not aware of the requirement to disable default accounts. No regular compliance verification mechanism existed for database baseline configurations.

Effective_Date:01-Apr-2024: Disable default sa account on all production SQL instances except where required for legacy application compatibility. For instances where sa is required
Entity:Bharat Mercantile Bank Limited: rename the account and apply a complex 25+ character password.

Effective_Date:01-Apr-2024: Deploy Azure AD Privileged Identity Management (PIM) for all DBA logins requiring MFA enforcement. Configure just-in-time (JIT) privileged access.
Implement mandatory 90-day password rotation policy for all database service accounts. Automate via CyberArk PAM.
Update Database Hardening Standard (STD-DB-012) to include MFA requirement
Conduct awareness training for all DBAs on privileged access management requirements and baseline configuration standards.
Verify sa account disabled or renamed on all 14 instances via SQL query.
Attempt DBA login using password only (no MFA) to validate Azure AD PIM MFA enforcement.
column5: Pass - MFA enforced for all DBA logins

Effective_Date:01-Apr-2024: Verify password age for all DB service accounts is < 90 days via automated script.
column5: Pass - All accounts compliant with rotation policy

column7: Pass - All three requirements included in version 2.1

This observation is closed with all five corrective actions verified. The root cause has been addressed via policy and standard updates. Preventive controls (automated password rotation and MFA) are in place. No recurrence expected. Observation met all closure criteria per SOP DI-02-02.
